key_highlights: Iran deal progress drives oil down and stocks up, but stagflation fears persist
Gold jumps on tempered inflation concerns, while yields remain elevated
Consumer costs for travel, housing, and energy remain high, with CD/savings rates near 4%
Tech sector shows resilience with NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Amazon near buy points
Geopolitical risks include Iran talks, Taiwan arms sale, and Congo Ebola outbreak
Bitcoin falls to one-month low amid $1B liquidations
New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh sworn in, rate cuts seen as unlikely

sectors.trend: Oil prices drop sharply on Iran deal optimism, but strategists warn yields and energy costs will stay high even if war ends. US oil nearly doubled YTD.
sectors.implications: Short-term relief for consumers and airlines, but structural supply issues and high volatility persist. Energy stocks may correct but remain attractive for income.

sectors.trend: AI and semiconductor stocks (NVIDIA, Broadcom, Micron, Astera Labs) continue to rally with strong earnings and buy points. Apple's growth narrative strengthens. Snap faces caution post-earnings.
sectors.implications: Tech remains a core growth driver. AI infrastructure spending (data centers, packaging) is accelerating. Selective buying on dips recommended.

sectors.trend: Gold jumps on Iran deal prospects and tempered inflation fears. Analysts see rangebound near term. A $10M bet on gold miners signals bullish sentiment.
sectors.implications: Gold remains a hedge against geopolitical and stagflation risks. ETFs with lower fees (0.10% vs 0.40%) offer significant long-term savings.

sectors.trend: Walmart is 'priced for perfection' while Target is preferred by former CEO. Home Depot struggles with customer trends. Travel costs high, but Memorial Day deals abound.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary faces headwinds from inflation and high energy costs. Value-oriented retailers and travel-related services may outperform.

sectors.trend: CD rates at 4% APY, high-yield savings at 4.1%. Yields expected to stay high. Japan bond surge deepens regional bank divide. ECB may revise inflation outlook.
sectors.implications: Income investors can lock in attractive rates. Bond market volatility may persist. Regional banks face margin pressure from rising rates.

sectors.trend: Iran talks show significant progress, Hormuz Strait reopening likely. Trump mulls Taiwan arms sale. India-US ties improve. Indonesia to clarify commodity export policy. Congo Ebola spreads.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium is easing for oil but rising for Taiwan/China. Commodity supply chains (coal, rare earths) remain volatile. Ebola poses humanitarian risk.

key_themes: Iran deal optimism driving risk-on rotation out of oil and into equities
Stagflation fears persist despite May data confirming slowdown
AI and tech infrastructure spending remains a dominant growth theme
Consumer cost pressures from energy, housing, and travel
Fixed income yields attractive but may not hedge equity shocks
Crypto selloff amid macro uncertainty and quantum threat
New Fed leadership signals no near-term rate cuts

risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain energy exposure (XLE, CVX) and gold (GLD) as hedges. Reduce cyclical exposure if talks stall.
Favor TIPS, commodities, and quality dividend stocks. Avoid long-duration bonds and high-beta consumer discretionary.
Reduce exposure to semiconductor supply chain (TSM, NVDA) if tensions spike. Hold cash or gold.
Limit crypto exposure. Quantum threat to encryption is long-term but growing. Monitor regulatory responses.
Monitor travel restrictions and commodity supply chains. No immediate market impact but could escalate.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Bond market shock (yields spike or inversion)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify fixed income with short-duration and floating rate. Use gold as alternative hedge.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold and low-fee gold ETFs (e.g., GLD, IAU) on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainty, stagflation fears, and potential Fed pivot support gold. Lower fees compound savings.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate AI/semiconductor leaders on dips (NVDA, AVGO, MU)
opportunities.rationale: Strong earnings, AI infrastructure spending, and buy points near highs. Citi bullish on MU DRAM surge.

opportunities.recommendation: Lock in 4%+ CD and high-yield savings rates
opportunities.rationale: Rates are attractive and may decline if Iran deal reduces inflation. Secure yield now.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider energy stocks with high dividends (XOM, CVX, ET)
opportunities.rationale: Oil may correct but structural underinvestment and high yields (5-7%) provide income and upside if supply tightens.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Amazon and Apple on strength
opportunities.rationale: Durable growth narratives, strong buy points, and AI/cloud tailwinds. AMZN near buy point.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to high-beta consumer discretionary and small caps
defensive_moves.rationale: Stagflation and high energy costs pressure consumer spending. Home Depot and Walmart show caution.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with put options on oil or energy ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil could drop sharply if Iran deal is finalized. Protect gains from YTD rally.
Geopolitical risks, stagflation, and potential market pullback (RBC sees 5-10% downside) warrant dry powder.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify into utility stocks (Canadian, US) for stable income
defensive_moves.rationale: Defensive sector with strong 2026 outlook, less sensitive to energy volatility.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets likely to remain volatile with a bullish bias if Iran deal is finalized. Oil could drop 10-15%, boosting equities. However, stagflation fears and high yields may cap gains. S&P 500 could see 5-10% pullback on any deal disappointment.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Gradual recovery if inflation moderates and Fed holds steady. AI and tech will lead. Geopolitical fragmentation (Taiwan, commodities) and climate/health risks (Ebola, heatwaves) add uncertainty. Gold and energy remain strategic holdings.
key_catalysts: Iran deal announcement and Hormuz Strait reopening
Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first policy signals
AI regulation and quantum computing threats to crypto
Commodity export policy from Indonesia and China
watch_list: Gold price and gold miner ETF (GDX)
NVIDIA, Broadcom, and Micron earnings momentum
India rupee and Japan bond yields
Memorial Day travel and consumer spending data
